page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a profile suited for moderate workloads. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. Geekbench testing shows an average single-core score of 1321.0, but a weak multi-core score of 3938.0. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate average cached read speeds of 410.01 GB/sec, though uncached large-block reads and writes are weaker at 34.61 GB/sec and 14.27 GB/sec respectively. Database and web serving capabilities are modest, with static web serving throughput at 3.08 GB/sec and Redis SET operations reaching 5.66 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of bundled local storage, this server offers a budget-friendly option for resource-dense, non-CPU-intensive applications. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as light web serving, basic text and image processing, small-scale LLM prototyping using models under 1B parameters, and development or testing environments where continuous full CPU utilization is not required.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, utilizing a shared AMD EPYC 7542 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Lacking bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is designed for budget-conscious general-purpose workloads. Benchmark data reveals moderate single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1321.0, alongside weaker multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth performance. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 3.08 GB/sec and 5.66 million Redis SET operations per second. This resource profile is highly cost-efficient for applications that do not demand sustained CPU performance, making it suitable for development environments, light web serving, basic image processing, and low-intensity background tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It is designed for budget-friendly general-purpose workloads and development environments.
